text.title: 48 HOURS in Madrid (Spain's GORGEOUS Capital!) 🇪🇸 Things to Do + Eat!
text.u: Madrid, the center of Spain,
geographically, politically, culturally.
This week, we want to learn about what
it really means to be a Madrileno. We
want to experience the palaces, the
plazas, the football, the hone. This is
eating good. This is sitting good. Thank
you, Madrid. But Madrid is not a new
place for me. The last time I came here,
it was 2007 on a family vacation. My
hair was bigger and my suitcase was half
Albert Comry polos. And I have never
been to a Spanish-sp speakaking country
outside of my home country of Mexico.
And this place surprised me in the way
it felt like home, but also slightly
different in the weirdest ways. The
different words they use like botros,
the food, and how delicious it all was.
I was hooked. Now I'm back years later
and I've got a big question on my mind.
Has Madrid changed?
Good morning, travelers. We made it to
Spain. You guys have been asking for
this series for I don't even know how
long. I know it's early. Madrid is
waking up and we're also waking up and
we're going to start this journey over
here in Plaza Dol. This is supposed to
be a 72-hour guide to Madrid. But
yesterday we had some flight
complications and now we have to do
everything we want to see here in 48
hours. So, high plaza and by plaza
because I have some churros I'm
desperately wanting to eat right now.
Let's go to get some Madrid breakfast
[Music]
priorities.
Okay.
Any day that we can start with churros
is going to be a good day. This has been
the meal I've been most excited for in
our nine-part series here in Europe. The
smell in this place is incredible. A lot
of people come to Madrid and they do
churros and chocolate elsewhere at a
very popular place. But this place is
super underrated. It's been familyowned
generations. This chocolate looks like
they've just taken a chocolate bar and
just melted it and served it to me. I'm
so excited to try this. And according to
Martine, you can dip them. So, no, you
must you must dip them. I've never done
that with a churro, so I'm very
excited. Wo!
That is some thick
chocolate. Holy Toledo. This is amazing.
Do we have breakfast plans tomorrow?
[Music]
We do. Ah, dang it. I want to come back
here. Maybe before we leave, before we
go on the plane. It's so good. We needed
to order more, guys, because that's too
little for great chocolate. And we
ordered these ones. These ones, they
don't call it churro. They call it
pores. It's fine to me. It's like a
churot churote churote.
[Music]
Which ones are better? Really? Dang it.
I ate almost that whole plate. This
one's because I think the chocolate
tastes more over there and it's less
crunching, but it's good for the
chocolate. Oh, interesting. Mhm. It's
safe to say that Cheria Chocolaria 1902
is definitely underrated. But our next
stop, well, no one's missing it. This is
where Madrid puts its biggest moments on
display. This one, this plaza right now
it's called Plaza Mayor, but it has been
called different names. Plaza de la
Raal, Plaza realal, Plaza de la
Constitution, Plaza de la Republica. So
many, many names. So this is nice
because normally there's a lot of people
sitting over here so you cannot see
this, but it tells you the story about
this place. It used to be bull fighting
over here. Also, they tell you the story
about the different fires in this in
this plaza. This one for instance is in
1790. Uh the name of the
architecta and there was also trials
over here but also carnivals. So it's a
very important plaza for the history of
Madrid. It's very good that we have to
see this before it's completely full of
people. There is something I was always
curious about in Madrid. I heard rumors
about cookies made from nuns in a 17th
century monastery called Monasterio the
corpos Christi. So, of course, we have
to find it out if it was real.
Where are you taking us?
Get some candies. Julina had questions.
Fair ones because yeah, there's no one
else over here. And telling her I saw on
the internet we can buy sweets in this
completely empty monastery does sounds
like a good plot for a horror movie. But
you need to trust me here. This is so
strange.
[Music]
What? You can buy this here in this
church? Definitely. There's no one
working here.
[Music]
What? Today, we'll be buying the cookies
without being face to face with anyone.
Instead, we'll use this wooden turn
style to buy what they call gayas de
conventto.
That has to be the craziest way I've
ever bought cookies in my life. Martin,
and look at that. We got the whole box
to take home with us. What kind do we
even get? I was so swept up in the
moment. It's almond. Oh my gosh, that's
going to be so good. We're going to try
these. But the thing is is we're
cramming 3 days worth of eating into
two. So, I think we're just going to
take it home as a souvenir. Definitely.
But that was a great experience. Yeah,
super cool. Across town is our next
spot. So, we hop the metro and head to
Park Delverro. Once a royal playground
for King Philip IV, now it's one of the
few places where Madrid actually slows
down. There's 15,000 trees, 350 acres,
and these cute little boats and ducks
that made us forget for a minute that
we're in the middle of a capital city.
[Music]
When we were planning Madrid, this was
over there in our plans. But then when
we knew we needed to shorten to 48
hours, we said, "Ah, we cannot put
this." Yeah. So, we weren't supposed to
be here. But guess what? When you have a
fire under your butt to get things
moving, sometimes you end up doing
things faster than you expected. So, we
had a little spot because we can't keep
eating food. We We're still full from
the churros and we're very glad we're
here because it looks amazing. Even
though I think the biggest attraction of
this park that is the Crystal Palace is
closed, still is a beautiful place to
be. The cemetery, the people on their
little boats and the ducks and the
little pond over here, the school kids
walking by. Yeah. People walking their
dogs. It's a beautiful, beautiful
morning. And I'm very grateful I'm alive
to see this beautiful park. That was
really sweet, honey. It's a It's a I
think it's a nice time to be alive over
here in Madrid in a morning like this.
It is. I agree. Okay, enough being
sentimental. We're hungry again. And
when lunchtime hits in Madrid, there's
only one place you got to go. Marcado
San Miguel. This place has been around
for over a century, but it wasn't like
this last time I was here. For a while,
it was falling apart and was almost
demolished entirely. But in 2009, it
came back to life, and now it's one of
the best examples of Madrid's revival
and a good place to eat. Oh my god, that
looks like something I want. Oh my god,
it smells so good in here. And we're
hungry, so that's the perfect
combination. Oh my gosh, we're like 5 ft
in and we already have three places on
our definitely want that list. I know.
Probably we're going to find more.
We get a lot of things here like this
sampler of various tortillas from a
stand called Picolisto and this monster
of a tapa at mozzarella bar. This place
is the epitome of girl dinner and I love
it. Lots of just little things to snack
on. But I am eyeing this bad
boy. Look at all this baratha cheese.
When I saw this, I knew immediately I
was going to inhale this in about 15
seconds flat. It looks so good. It's my
favorite. This one, it's called the
Martin because it has it's not called
like that, but I name it like that
because it has everything that Martin
likes. Olive, dried tomato, cheese,
pickle. Come on. I'm going to start
doing this whenever I watching football
at home. Yeah. Honestly, I don't even
know how to eat this. Oh, it's going to
fall. Okay.
Oh my god. There's like this sweet
tomato jam on top and so much bara. They
know me. This is like an entire bara
portion in one bite. So good. I'm going
to try this. And for a Mexican like
myself, I have to tell you the tortilla
here is very different because it's made
of potato and eggs like the one at
home. The bread is toasted and then the
tortilla patata is not well done, which
I like. And you don't mean not well done
like bad. You mean not like super
completely cooked. Exactly. That's the
way it has to be. And also on top it was
like a little sauce. I thought it was
tomato, but no, it was a little spicy,
which I love. Now, don't tell the other
countries or the other cities. But for
me personally, Madrid is in the top five
cities in the entire world where you eat
the best. It's amazing. It's amazing.
It's amazing. You know what I'm saying?
And I'm enjoying I'm in heaven right
now. Now, one of the biggest things we
were warned about before coming to Spain
was pickpocketing, especially in Madrid
and Barcelona. Every local we met had a
story. And once you've been in a crowded
market like this, you get it. So, some
tips we always tell our viewers. Keep
your phone in zip pockets. Use cross
body bags and keep them on you at all
times. And turn your backpacks to the
front in busy areas. But here's
something nobody talks about. Digital
pick pocketing. Today's video is brought
to you by NordVPN, who keeps us safe
online while we travel. Because just
like someone can reach into your pocket
in real life, someone can reach into
your device on an open network and grab
your passwords, banking details, even
your location. We've been guilty of
clicking any free Wi-Fi we find. But
sometimes that Wi-Fi is a little too
free. But it's not just cafes. Even
hotel Wi-Fi or airport networks can be
risky, especially whenever you're
booking flights or checking your bank
account. that data is just floating
around for anybody to grab it if you're
not protected. That's why we never log
into public Wi-Fi anymore without a VPN.
We use NordVPN because it keeps
everything encrypted, even if the Wi-Fi
isn't. And it's not just about safety.
When we're abroad, websites sometimes
block access just because we're not in
our home country, like airline sites
showing different prices or even our
favorite streaming networks not working.
With NordVPN, we can switch our location
and use the internet like we're back
home. So, yeah, if you're traveling,
we're remote, stream content, or you
want peace of mind when you hop online,
NordVPN is what you need. Go to
nordvpn.com/local for a 2-year plan,
plus four extra months at a huge
discount risk-free with Nord's 30-day
guarantee. Madrid is a funny city
because there's a lot of little details
that you can find on top of the doors,
on top of the buildings, you know,
there's there's certain things. You
really have to look it up for those
those kind of details that makes this
city special. For example, over on Grand
Via, Madrid's central hub for shopping
and Broadway shows, you'll find some
pretty crazy details if you just look
up, like this statue of a Roman goddess
shooting down a phoenix on the other
side of the street. And if you look
down, the pavement below actually has a
print of the arrows, too. It's fun stuff
like these that made me love this place
whenever I first visited. And speaking
of fun, I know a neighborhood for that.
We decided to move over to Lacha. This
is a neighborhood you want to party. Let
me tell you that we are not very party
people. At least not anymore.
So, we are here just exploring and
finding a place to
[Music]
eat. I remember when I came here with my
family a lot of years ago, this
neighborhood, it looked so different. It
had a lot of personality and definitely
it changed through the years. Yeah.
Unfortunately, you know, I think this
neighborhood is for someone. It just
maybe isn't for us anymore. And that
could be largely due to the fact that we
have traveled to a lot of neighborhoods
like this recently through our travels.
You know, the gentrified neighborhood of
the city. There's lots of coffee shops
here and lots of cute bars. So, it's
definitely a good place to pass by those
hours before dinner. But other than
that, is it worth going out of your way
to go through here? I don't necessarily
think so. H Yeah, exactly. As you said,
Mamor, it's only to have a drink with
someone of your friends or like hang
around a little bit. And speaking of
drinks with your friends, there's a cool
bar on the other side of the city that
is well different than anything we'd
find in Lewa. And one thing you need to
know about us is that we personally are
suckers for any kind of themed
bar. We're at this place that is called
La Peria. That means the hardware store.
And we wanted to eat something over
here, but we didn't know that the
kitchen is closed certain hours. And we
walk so far to get over here. But well,
at least we're having a drink and we're
admiring this place because it's
something else. Along all the wall,
there's certain things that you will buy
in a place like this, you know. We're
not regretting it, but still, we need
something to eat. I did the research.
This place supposed to have a very good
food, but well, the kitchen is closed.
What we can do? We decide to wander a
bit when we come across a place that
doesn't look like much from outside, but
inside it's packed with locals fresh out
of work eating some delicious and
affordable tapas. This looks like our
type of place. We really wanted to live
the experience of tapas and we found the
right place to do it. This place is
called Los. The menu is extensive and I
knew this was going to be a good spot
because when we sat down, they threw
these delicious sausages in front of us,
like smoked. So so smoky and melt in
your mouth. They were so good. I I want
to figure out what they were and was
without asking. It was like welcome, you
know. It was amazing. So in front of us
we have several tapas, but we're going
to just keep ordering more and more and
more. These look delicious. I'm so glad
we're here. Yeah. No frills, no lines,
just locals having tapas. And that's
exactly what we wanted here in
Madrid.
[Music]
Another day in Madrid. I'm excited for
day two. I think it's going to be a very
different day that it was yesterday. We
have a lot of fun things to do today.
Yeah. We're starting off here in Plaza
de Espa. What a gorgeous place. And uh
what do you say we take a look through
here before we head to our next stop,
which is a museo. A museum, which is a
museo. A museum like you've never seen
before.
Sorry. What? A museum. A museum.
Shut up. You know, we make these videos
in English and Spanish. I get a little
stuck sometimes. I love it. I love it.
Anyway, let's go to the
mo a mus. Okay, here's a here's a tip
for you. Don't learn English from me.
Okay, today we aren't visiting a museum
with ancient sculptures or dusty oil
paintings. This is a temple of taste, a
monument to the noble pig. Welcome to
Elmo del Hammon. Inside, legs of ham
dangle from the ceiling like holy
relics. And the energy is part Delhi,
part fever
[Music]
dream. we're talking about and it has a
very special place in my heart over here
because every single day when we were in
Madrid with my family that is the best
that I remember with them. We always
wanted to have a breakfast over here but
it was this juice a sandwich and a
coffee every single day before we start
exploring beautiful Madrid. We're having
this and right now it's hard being over
here without them because I live now in
the US. So, it's difficult for me to see
them or it's difficult for me to have a
trip with them, especially a great one
like it was in Madrid. So, it just
brings me back. This reminds me. So, the
first thing that it was in my plans
whenever we knew we're coming over here
to Madrid was having this breakfast. And
I don't want to cry whenever I have the
first bite, but probably I will. It's
it's this is this is very important for
me. and I will send a picture of course
to my family. I love you guys.
[Music]
As good as I remember, I meet my family.
Guys, what are you doing down there?
Come here with us. We're in the Royal
Palace of Madrid. This is the What did
you say? Largest palace in accidental
Europe. Is that correct? That's correct.
almost doubles the size of Versailles
and also Buckingham Palace. And if
you're planning to come over here, book
your tickets in advance because the line
is quite shorter if you do that. Yeah,
for sure. Let me go take a look inside.
Let's go. Technically, this palace is
the official residence of the Spanish
royal family. But no one is actually
living here these days. It's just used
for state ceremonies. And this place is
massive. There's over 3,400 rooms across
135,000 square m. It cost €14 to enter
here. And even though it's crowded, it's
one of the spots we think it's worth
[Music]
it. I mean, this is palace elegana
extravaganza to the max. Everything is
so ornate. It's breathtaking. And
Martinez was just telling me something
funny that I can definitely relate to.
Um he was saying that when he visited
here with his family, he did not
appreciate this place as a 17-year-old.
And that's the funny thing about travel
is you could go to a place at a
different period of your life and have a
different takeaway of it. This place is
really cool. And I'm realizing that
maybe we need to redecorate our
apartment. Honey,
we need a bigger budget. See? Very, very
nice to live over here. It's beautiful.
The only con it will be like you're in
one side and it's like, "Oh my god, I
forgot my cell phone in the other. You
have to walk all the way over there." So
now I'm good in my I think that I think
that people who live here um they have
they have people for those types of
tasks. That's true. Time for lunch. And
we're not going to lie, we tried to come
to this place yesterday, but we
couldn't. So if you try to come to this
place, please make a reservation. So,
this place is called La Rosaria and it
specialized on rices. So, I bet it's
going to be a very good
[Music]
lunch. This is eating good. This is
sitting good. Thank you, Mad.
[Music]
This is creeping hot because it's quite
literally just come out of the kitchen.
They warn you when you order this that
it's going to take 30 minutes for it to
come out. That's how I knew this was a
good spot because everything is fresh.
Now, this is not paella. Okay? I know a
lot of tourists will see this and say,
"Oh, it's paella." Not everything with
rice is paella. Do they serve a really
good paella here? Yes. Probably the best
in the city. But what we ordered today
was one of their famous rice dishes.
This is packed with all sorts of meat,
an entire clove of garlic. And I kid you
not, even just the rice itself is so
flavorful. And what's fun is that this
is big enough to share for two people.
So, we are enjoying this lunch Madrid
style.
You guys didn't think we're going to be
in Madrid and not seeing the Santiago
Vabo, right? Is there anyone who's
surprised that we're here right now?
because I am not. Martin is a
notoriously huge football fan. So, of
course, we needed to do a stadium tour
of where Real Madrid plays. For €35
each, we got to walk through this huge
museum and we learned all about the
stadium itself and got to see all the
trophies. It's very rare that I, someone
who does not watch sports, gets very
impressed with the stadium. But check
this out, guys. They can actually flip
the field that's in the middle. That's
really cool. I mean, it's amazing.
People are going absolutely nuts over
all of this stuff. I'm excited for them.
Even though I don't know much about
football, I'm trying. What's that?
Martinez is indoctrinating me into the
football world. So, slowly I'm learning.
And, you know, everybody's just so happy
to be here. Yeah. Especially in this
room because there's the 15 Champions
League of Real Madrid, which is it's a
big deal. I I don't know much about
football, but I do know that that's a
lot. Yeah, definitely. I'm glad I got to
see the stadium, but also I really want
to be real with you. We have done much
better stadium tours in other places
where we have a guide and it cost much
lower. Super fans of Real Madrid will
still love this experience, but it's
probably worth skipping if seeing the
stadium isn't a huge goal of yours. But
I will say I was happy that at the end
we have as much time as we wanted to
take pictures inside of the stadium.
There's something else. Definitely
something else. And yes, of course, I'm
wearing my Puma jersey because Sanchez,
you know, I came back to Madrid
expecting change, and there has been
some. I noticed more tourists, trendier
bars, and a few new corners I didn't
know. But what's wild is how much it
hasn't changed at all. The buildings
there are still grand, the Hamon, the
still salty. Actually, Madrid didn't
change much. I did. Back then, I didn't
know how rare it was to travel with your
whole family. I didn't realize how
precious time was. I wonder where I will
be in life next time I'm in Madrid. All
right, enough of that. We have one last
meal over here. Well, we did it more. We
made it to our last spot. Hey,
definitely, but I don't want to leave. I
don't want to leave. I don't either. We
really liked Madrid. These last two
days, it was a great experience over
here in the capital of Spain, and
definitely it's a place that I will come
back. Now, this video focused a lot on
tradition, you know, seeing seeing the
real Madrid. Yeah. The culture of
Madrid. But there's one spot, as we
said, our flight got delayed. So, the
first day we arrived over here very
late. So, we just walk over here, the
Gambia, and we were looking for some
dinner and we found this spot. Yeah,
this was not even supposed to make it
into the itinerary, you guys. It's
totally different from something we
would normally show in our videos
because this is a very popular spot on
social media, right? But guess what? It
deserves a spot in this video because it
was that good. We've been dreaming about
returning. So unlike us, but we're going
to show you guys what is it. It's a
pasta place. It's amazing. And we're
going to have dinner over here. A good
dinner. We're probably going to order
the exact same thing we did the other
night. Definitely. This is Bea Pasta.
The inside looks like a spaceship. And
you can look through the spaceship
windows to watch them making the fresh
pasta. It's not like any Italian
restaurant we've ever seen before.
Martin loves the Tartufo Fest, which is
like Kacowi Pepe, but served straight
from a cheese wheel. And I went for the
candy me, which is actually barata
stuffed pasta in a pecarino cream and
tomato sauce. That is life-changing.
And that's the reason why I suggesting
this place. Not only because they have
freshmade pasta every single day. Also
because they serve it in such fun ways.
You see how they serve my pasta? And
also Juliana is made like a little
candies. Not only it's fun how they make
it, it tastes delicious. Delicious,
guys. It's one of the best pastas we
have. And what I really wanted to come
back for was their incredible I think
it's called the habibi cake dessert. It
is Dubai chocolate which I had never had
before coming here. And you guys need to
hear the crunch on this thing.
So good. It's like I don't even know
what's in this. I know there's pistachio
and chocolate and so much whipped cream
as you guys can see. There's magic. So
good. Next week we're headed to Sevilla.
We cannot wait to share that video with
you guys. But in the meantime, we're
going to leave you one video so you can
watch our adventures. So long, travel
well. Make the world your neighborhood.
See you guys next time.
